messages.content: Sure, I'd be happy to help translate something for you. However, it looks like the text that you want to be translated is missing from your message. Could you please provide the text you need to be translated into English?

축구 천재가 아니라는 걸 들키기 싫다면 말이다.

ㆍㆍㆍ

“감독님.”

“응?”

“좋은 소식!”

“뭔데?”

훈련이 끝난 뒤.

U17 팀의 비주전조를 담당하는 루카 코치가 호들갑을 떨며 말한다.

“그, 지안 있잖습니까.”

“어. 왜?”

“오늘따라 좀 이상하던데요?”

“뭐가?”

“아니, 열심히 하더라고요. 그냥 열심히 하는 수준이 아니라, 무슨 이를 악물고 하던데요.”

“···이지안이?”

“예. 무슨 일 있었던 걸까요? 아니면 슬슬 차도가 보이는 건가?”

한국에서 온 요주의 인물, 이지안이 성실하다 못해 눈에 불을 켜고 훈련에 임했다는 루카 코치의 말에, U17 팀의 감독 토니 루초가 턱을 매만지며 고개를 끄덕인다.

“으음.”

무슨 일이길래 호들갑을 떠나 했더니, 호들갑 떨 만한 일이긴 했다.

이지안이 성실히 훈련에 임했다? 이건 빅 뉴스가 맞다.

아닌 게 아니라, 이 16살짜리 동양인 꼬맹이는 코치들 모두가 가장 신경 쓰는 요주의 인물이었으니까.

사실 팀에 들어오기 전부터 코치들을 긴장시킨 게 바로 이지안이었다.

배경은 이렇다.

이지안이라는 선수의 존재를 알게 된 건 3년 전, 한국의 한 유튜버가 진행한 프로그램에서였다.

당시 세리에 몇몇 클럽의 관계자들이 모여, 한국의 어린 유망주들을 테스트해주는 취지의 프로그램이었는데.

그 프로그램에서 군계일학이었던 게 이지안이었다.

그때 참가했던 세리에 팀들 모두가 녀석을 원픽으로 뽑을 정도로 말이다.

프로그램 후반엔 오히려 세리에 클럽들이 안달을 낼 지경이었다.

결국 이지안을 데려간 건 유벤투스였다.

피오렌티나도 입찰을 넣어보긴 했지만, 뭐.

상대가 유벤투스였으니 체념하는 수밖에 없었는데.

올해 초, 이지안이 유벤투스와 계약 해지에 상호 합의했다는 소식을 전해 들었다.

믿을만한 정보통에 의하면, 이유는 이랬다.

이지안이 이탈리아 생활에 적응을 어려워한다는 것이었다.

언어적으로, 문화적으로.

갑자기 하게 된 해외 생활에 적응하지 못하고 겉돌았다는 것이다.

심지어 동료 선수들과 싸움을 했다는 얘기도 있었다.

사실, 어느 정도 예견은 가능했던 일이었다.

3년 전 당시 평가하기로.

축구적 재능은 의심의 여지가 없지만, 성격적인 면에선 약간의 불안 요소가 보였던 이지안이기 때문이었다.

뭐랄까.

인간이라는 게 워낙 다중복합적인 생물체라 이지안의 성격을 한마디로 정의 내릴 순 없지만.

확실한 건 치열한 경쟁을 즐기는 타입은 아니라는 것이었다.

이지안은 그라운드 밖에선 엄청나게 부끄러움이 많은 아이였다.

주목을 받는 것도 싫어하고, 굉장히 내성적이었다. 특히 부담과 압박을 받는 걸 병적으로 싫어한다고도 들었다.

이건 당시 이지안을 지도하던 축구부 코치에게 들은 얘기다.

그가 말하길, 한번은 대회 결승전을 앞두고 이지안을 불러 ‘네가 해줘야 한다, 네가 잘해주지 않으면 이길 수 없다, 할 수 있겠느냐’는 식으로 동기부여를 해준 적이 있다고 한다.

근데, 그 말을 들은 이지안이 그 자리에서 구토를 했단다.

거의 경기를 일으키는 수준이었다고.

그 이유에 대해선 확실하게 알 수 없으나.

그 코치가 추측하는 게 있기는 했다.

이지안의 어머니에 관한 이야기였다.

이지안의 어머니는 매 경기를 보러 올 정도로 열정적인 보호자였는데.

문제는 경기가 끝나면 항상 이지안을 불러 혼을 냈다고 한다.

심지어 무척 잘한 경기에서도, 그 정도로는 안 된다, 최고의 선수가 되려면 더 잘해야 한다는 식으로 혼을 냈다고.

심지어 아이들이 다 듣는 앞에서 말이다.

그 코치에게 그걸 보고만 있었냐고 했더니, 뭐라더라.

한국에선 학부모 건드리면 큰일 난다고 어쩔 수 없었다 했나.

뭐, 이건 여담이고.

아무튼··· 이지안은 그런 아이였기에 해외 생활에 잘 적응할 수 있을지 의문이긴 했다.

재능에 비해 자존감과 자신감이 현저히 부족한 스타일.

상당히 세심한 케어가 필요한 케이스였던 건데, 워낙 유망주가 즐비한 유벤투스라 아마 그런 부분에서 좀 소홀했던 것이 아닐까 싶다.

이야기가 좀 길게 돌아왔는데.

어쨌든 피오렌티나는 그 소식을 입수하자마자 접근해 이지안을 데려왔다.

그리고 이지안을 어떻게 케어할지 회의까지 벌여 방침을 세웠다.

사실 특별할 건 없었다.

그저 기다려주는 것.

너무 과도한 관심을 주기보단, 오히려 관심을 거두고 뒤에서만 지켜보자는 것이었다.

녀석이 먼저 마음의 문을 열 수 있게끔 말이다.

“슬슬 마음을 여는 건가?”

“그런 거라면 더할 나위 없죠.”

“그랬으면 좋겠네. 루카.”

“예.”

“혹시라도 뭐 다르게 행동하지 말고. 하던 대로 해.”

“알죠. 그냥 지켜만 보겠습니다. 평소처럼, 무심하게.”

토니 감독이 고개를 끄덕였다.

불행하게도 이지안은 거의 2년을 버렸다.

유벤투스에선 경기도 제대로 나서지 못했으니.

하지만 16살의 나이는 여전히 어리기만 했다.

녀석이 가진 재능은 의심할 여지가 없기에··· 다시 마음의 문을 열고 축구에 집중한다면.

피오렌티나의 미래는 녀석에 의해 달라질 수도 있는 일이었다.

If you don't want to be found out as not being a soccer genius, that is.

...

"Coach."

"Yes?"

"Good news!"

"What is it?"

After training.

Coach Luca, who is in charge of the U17 team's non-regular squad, speaks excitedly.

"That, Gian is there."

"Yeah. What about him?"

"He was a bit strange today?"

"What do you mean?"

"Well, he was working hard. Not just working hard, but like he was grinding his teeth."

"...Gian?"

"Yes. Was there something going on? Or is he starting to show some progress?"

Tony Luccho, the coach of the U17 team, strokes his chin and nods his head at Coach Luca's words that the Korean player of interest, Gian, was training with fiery determination.

"Hmm."

It was a fuss worth making after all.

Gian trained diligently? That's definitely big news.

It's not for nothing, this 16-year-old Asian kid was the most concerning figure for all the coaches.

In fact, it was Gian who had made the coaches nervous even before he joined the team.

Here's the background.

It was three years ago when they first learned of a player named Gian through a program conducted by a Korean YouTuber.

At that time, officials from several Serie A clubs gathered to test young Korean prospects in a program with that purpose.

In that program, Gian stood out as the best of the best.

He was the top pick for all the Serie A teams that participated.

Towards the end of the program, the Serie A clubs were almost desperate to have him.

In the end, it was Juventus who took Gian.

Fiorentina also made a bid, but well.

Since it was Juventus, they had no choice but to give up.

Earlier this year, I heard that Gian had mutually agreed to terminate his contract with Juventus.

According to reliable sources, the reason was as follows:

Gian was having trouble adapting to life in Italy.

Linguistically, culturally.

He couldn't adapt to the sudden overseas life and felt out of place.

There were even rumors that he had fought with his teammates.

In fact, it was somewhat predictable.

Three years ago, according to the evaluation.

There was no doubt about Gian's football talent, but there were some concerns about his personality.

How should I put it?

Humans are such complex creatures that it's hard to define Gian's personality in one word.

But one thing was certain: he was not the type to enjoy fierce competition.

Gian was extremely shy off the field.

He disliked being the center of attention and was very introverted. Especially, he was said to have a pathological dislike of being under pressure and stress.

This was a story I heard from a soccer coach who used to coach Gian.

He said that once, before a final match, he called Gian and tried to motivate him by saying, 'You have to do it, we can't win unless you play well, can you do it?'

But after hearing that, Gian vomited on the spot.

It was almost to the point of causing a scene.

The exact reason is unclear.

But the coach had a theory.

It was about Gian's mother.

Gian's mother was a passionate guardian who came to watch every game.

The problem was that she always scolded Gian after the games.

Even after a very good game, she would say that it wasn't enough, that he needed to do better to become the best player.

Even in front of other children.

When I asked the coach why he just watched it happen, what did he say?

In Korea, if you touch a parent, it's a big problem, so he couldn't help it.

Well, that's an aside.

Anyway... there were doubts about whether Gian, being such a child, could adapt well to life abroad.

A style with significantly low self-esteem and confidence compared to his talent.

He was a case that needed quite careful care, but perhaps Juventus, with its abundance of promising players, had been somewhat negligent in that regard.

The story has gone a bit long, but anyway.

As soon as Fiorentina got wind of this news, they approached and brought Gian over.

And they even held a meeting on how to care for Gian and set a policy.

In fact, there was nothing special.

Just waiting.

Instead of giving too much attention, they decided to withdraw their interest and just watch from behind.

To let him open his heart first.

"Is he starting to open up?"

"If that's the case, it couldn't be better."

"I hope so. Luca."

"Yes."

"Just don't act differently. Keep doing what you're doing."

"I know. I'll just watch. Indifferently, as usual."

Coach Tony nodded.

Unfortunately, Gian had almost wasted two years.

He hadn't been able to play properly at Juventus.

But at the age of 16, he was still young.

There was no doubt about the talent he possessed... If he could open his heart again and focus on soccer.

The future of Fiorentina could be changed by him.

오히려 전진 패스를 받아야 하는 2선 자원에 문제가 있을 확률이 더 높다.

패스를 줄 수 있는 위치에 있어야 전진 패스를 주든 말든 할 테니까.

따라서 내가 활로를 찾아내지 못한다면 이 상황이 고착될 수밖에 없다는 건데.

심지어 나는 깍두기다.

2선 자원들의 움직임에 문제가 있다 친다면, 그게 기존 주전들의 문제일 확률이 높을까 아니면 깍두기로 들어온 나의 문제일 확률이 높을까.

당연히 두 번째겠지.

설령 실제로 그렇지 않더라도 그렇게 ‘여겨지기가’ 쉬울 테고.

한마디로, 횡 패스나 백 패스가 한 번씩 늘어날 때마다 제일 초조해지는 건 나라는 얘기다.

1초라도 빨리 정답인 위치를 찾아, 그곳에 서 있어야 한다.

정답에 가까운 위치여서도 안 된다.

비주전인 나로서는 꼭 정답만을 골라야 한다.

한가지 다행인 점은, 밖에서 경기를 지켜보며 미리 문제 풀이를 어느 정도 해놨다는 점일 거다.

일단은 내게 붙은 이 마크맨을 끌고 다니며 공간을 여는 것부터가 풀이의 시작이다.

타타탓-!

포워드의 영역을 과감히 버리고 중원으로 내려간다. 그러면서 뒤를 슬쩍 살피니, 마크맨 역시 따라붙는 게 보인다.

지노도 아니고 고작 나를 맨마킹 해야 하나 싶었는지 살짝 멈칫하는 게 보이긴 했는데.

아랑곳하지 않고 계속 내려가, 우리 팀 미드필더들 사이에 자리를 잡는다.

자, 일단 내가 수비 하나를 이끌고 내려왔으니 상대 진영 쪽에 공간 하나를 만들었다.

이젠 누군가가 그 공간을 먹어줘야 하는데···

“공간! 들어가!”

역시 주전조 아이들이다.

굳이 내가 말하지 않아도, 내 옆에 있던 미드필더 하나가 전방을 향해 뛰는 게 보였다.

이렇게 되면 여기서부턴 퍼즐 맞추기 게임이다.

빈공간을 향해 뛰는 녀석을 놔둘 순 없으니, 누군가는 움직일 터.

이럼 그 ‘누군가’가 지키고 있던 원래 자리가 다시 비게 되고, 그 자리를 다시 아군의 누군가가 먹어버릴 것이다.

이 물고 물리는 연쇄 이동이 이어지다 보면, 결국엔 빈틈이 생길 수밖에 없다.

하지만 중요한 건 빈틈이 생길 거라는 걸 예측하는 게 아니라, 그 빈틈이 어느 곳에 생길지를 예측하는 것.

타타탓-!

나 역시 계속해서 움직이며 주변을 끊임없이 살핀다. 동시에 머릿속으론 장기 말들을 움직여가며 시뮬레이션을 돌린다.

그렇게 시뮬레이션을 돌리다가··· 마침내 계산을 마치는 순간.

타타탓-!

왼쪽 하프 스페이스를 향해 뛴다.

내 계산이 맞았다면 결국 프리한 상황을 맞이하게 되는 건 왼쪽 공간.

그리고 그 공간으로 볼이 투입되기 위해선 왼쪽 중원을 거쳐 가야 한다.

그게 내가 왼쪽을 향해 달리는 이유.

만약 내 계산이 틀리지 않았다면, 공은 이리로 올 것이다.

과연 나는 정답을 골랐을까?

파아아앙-!

온다.

공이 온다.

드디어 전진 패스가 나왔고, 그 패스가 내게로 향하고 있었다.

일단은 정답을 맞췄다.

하지만 기뻐하기엔 이르다.

난 어디까지나 징검다리일 뿐.

최종 목표를 향해 빠르고 정확하게 공을 넘겨줄 의무가 남아있다.

패스가 내게 오는 방향, 즉 우리 쪽 골대를 바라보고 있던 나는,

뻐어어어엉-!

논스톱으로 공을 꺾어 찼다.

왼쪽 사이드를 향해 찌르는 패스.

어차피 계산도 끝났고, 내 뒤엔 여전히 마크맨이 따라오고 있으니.

잡아두고 찰 시간은 없었다.

슈우우우웅-

내 패스가 왼쪽으로 뻗어져 나가고.

나는 그 패스를 감상할 틈도 없이 다시 달린다.

패스를 연결 시켰다고 내 임무가 끝난 건 또 아니다.

난 어디까지나 공격수. 끝까지 박스를 향해 침투해야 한다.

다만 내가 헤더에 강점이 있는 건 아니니까, 컷백을 받을 수 있는 위치를 향해 뛰어야 한다.

“후우, 후우!”

호흡을 강하게 뱉어내며 이를 악물고 달린다.

사실 발이 내 맘대로 움직이지 않았다.

이놈의 저질 체력도 문제인데, 잠깐 사이에 뇌를 너무 썼는지 어질어질한 느낌마저 들었다.

하지만 나는 계속해서 달릴 수 있었다.

일단,

파아앙-!

내가 보낸 패스가 정확히 도달해, 와이드 오픈 찬스를 만들어냈다는 사실이 내게 힘을 주었고.

무엇보다··· 여기서 멈추면 지우에게 했던 거짓말들이 모두 들통날지도 모른다는 무서움이 내 다리를 움직이게 만들었다.

나 참.

거짓말이라는 게 이렇게나 무섭다.

If there's a problem with receiving forward passes, it's more likely to be with the second-line players who should be receiving them.

Because you have to be in a position to give a forward pass in order to decide whether to give it or not.

So if I can't find a way through, this situation will inevitably become fixed.

And I'm just a side dish.

If there's a problem with the movement of the second-line players, is it more likely to be an issue with the existing starters or with me, the side dish who just joined?

Obviously, it's the latter.

Even if it's not actually the case, it's easier to be 'perceived' that way.

In short, every time there's a lateral or back pass, I'm the one who gets the most anxious.

I need to find the correct position as quickly as possible and be there.

Tap-tap-tap!

I boldly abandon the forward's territory and drop back into the midfield. As I do so, I sneak a peek behind me and see that the marker is following me.

Now, since I've drawn one defender down with me, I've created a space in the opponent's territory.

Now someone needs to exploit that space...

As expected of the starters.

From here on, it's a puzzle game.

They can't just leave the guy running towards the empty space, so someone will have to move.

Then the 'someone's' original spot will be open again, and another ally will take that spot.

This chain of chasing and being chased will eventually create a gap.

But what's important is not predicting that a gap will appear, but predicting where that gap will appear.

Tap-tap-tap!

I also keep moving, constantly scanning my surroundings, while simulating moves in my head like chess pieces.

After running the simulation...

Finally, when I finish calculating.

Tap-tap-tap!

I sprint towards the left half-space.

If my calculations are correct, the left space will eventually be free.

And for the ball to be played into that space, it has to go through the left midfield.

That's why I'm running to the left.

If my calculations are not wrong, the ball will come this way.

Did I choose the right answer?

Whooosh!

Here it comes.

The ball is coming.

Finally, a forward pass is made, and it's heading towards me.

For now, I've chosen the right answer.

But it's too early to be happy.

I'm just a stepping stone.

I have a duty to pass the ball quickly and accurately towards the final goal.

Facing our goal, as the pass comes to me,

Whooosh!

Since I've finished calculating and still have a marker behind me.

Swoosh!

My pass stretches out to the left.

Just because I've connected the pass doesn't mean my job is done.

I'm a striker after all. I have to penetrate towards the box until the end.

Since I'm not strong with headers, I need to run towards a position where I can receive a cutback.

I breathe heavily and grit my teeth as I run.

In truth, my legs weren't moving as I wanted.

Whooosh!

And above all... the fear that if I stopped now, all the lies I told Jiwoo might be exposed, drove my legs to move.

"Pass it forward!"

"Push up the line!"

Whooosh-

Tap-tap-tap!

I decide to cut straight through.

I'm already at speed.

Rather than showing off unnecessary tricks, I decide it's better to use this speed for a quick resolution.

I see one of the opponent's midfielders trying to block my path. But to me, already at speed, he's no different than a training cone.

Tap-tap-tap!

I slightly change direction to bypass him.

Then I push the ball straight ahead again, closing the distance to the box.

Now the defenders start to close in on me.

With a desperate mindset to get past before the door closes, I charge through.

Can I make it?

Tap-tap-tap!

I learn once again that nothing is impossible if you're desperate enough.

I speed through the defense and enter the box.

To the right of the goal.

I see the goalkeeper hugging the near post, narrowing the angle.

Seeing his movement, I take a deep breath and charge at the ball.

Then, pulling back my right foot,

Whoooom!

I shoot.

Swoooosh-

Having put all my weight into the shot while at speed, I was sent tumbling to the ground.

After rolling around a couple of times,

I prop myself up and lift my head.

I see our team's kids screaming and running towards me.

But still... I can't help it. I have to pay for the time I've wasted.

And school starts in a few days, so I want to focus hard before then.

그리고 가방을 뒤적이더니 적당히 낡은 수첩 하나를 꺼냈다.

수첩을 펴든 그는 눈을 가늘게 뜨고 무언가를 찾기 시작했다.

“어디 보자··· 페··· 페···”

빼곡히 적힌 글자들을 훑으며 지나가던 그의 손이 한 페이지에서 멈춘다.

“···여기 있구만. 페데리코 키에사.”

페데리코 키에사.

피오렌티나 유소년 출신이자 현재는 유벤투스에서 뛰고 있는 이탈리아의 신성.

파올로 디렉터의 수첩엔 키에사의 연봉 협상 내역이 고스란히 적혀 있었다.

-2015년 주급 192유로.

-2016년 주급 2,385유로.

-2017년 주급 13,865유로.

-2018년 주급 60,538유로.

그 기록들을 읽어 내려가던 파올로 디렉터의 머릿속에 토니 감독이 했던 말이 떠오른다.

“오늘이 제일 쌉니다.”

푸흐흐- 너털웃음을 터뜨린 파올로 디렉터는 어디론가 급히 전화를 걸기 시작했다.

자금이 더 필요할 듯싶었다.

And I focus on just one person in front of me.

If I can get past just one person, it will make things much easier for our team.

Tap-tap-tap!

I set my direction to the right and cut diagonally.

The defense is also drawn in that direction, but then...

Tap!

I feint a change of direction by stamping my left foot on the ground and jolting my upper body. The opponent flinches at the movement.

Seizing that brief moment,

Whooosh!

I accelerate again, continuing in the right direction. It's not grand or flashy, but... nothing is as efficient as simplicity.

Tap-tap-tap!

I keep pulling him along, not letting him think about catching up. Then, once enough distance is secured, I slow down and wait, checking the positions of my teammates, until one more person comes to me.

Should I keep going if they don't come?

Tap-tap!

Yeah, they should come.

The opposing defensive midfielder approaches as if he can't just watch.

That means his original position is now vacant.

Our right winger, Macheda, breaks into that space.

Whooosh!

After threading a forward pass to Macheda, I immediately run towards the right.

Meanwhile, Macheda keeps the ball.

Once I'm in position, he returns the pass.

Whooosh!

I receive the ball with the touchline at my back.

Then I face the opposing fullback one-on-one... but the defensive midfielder I had shaken off is coming in from the left to help defend.

No, go away.

Whooosh!

I pass the ball back to our fullback supporting me, forcing the opponent to turn back,

Whooosh!

and then I receive the return pass.

Now it's truly one-on-one.

Before it becomes one-on-two again, I quickly charge at the opponent. All those times I faced off against Coach Luca were for this moment.

I trust myself and boldly advance.

Tap, tap-

I tap the ball with the instep of my right foot, showing my rhythm to the opponent, then...

Swoosh-

With a slight offbeat, I swing my left leg.

A feint with a step-over, adding upper body movement.

Can he withstand this dribble that Coach Luca and I have honed?

Hesitation-!

Right, there's no way he's better at defending than Coach Luca.

Move aside.

Tap-tap-tap!

As his balance breaks, I knock the ball to the right and break through. I successfully enter inside the box in an instant.

I continue to drive forward... but the angle seems too narrow for a shot.

I glance briefly towards the center.

I see our center forward, Enzo, charging towards the goal.

The keeper is also sticking to the near post, but seems to be in an ambiguous position, mindful of the cross.

This is...

Let's shoot.

Whoooom!

As hard as I can with the instep.

I strike with the thought of hitting the near post.

A delightful sensation travels up from my right foot.

Swoooosh-

I watch the shot soar towards the goal, and beyond it, I see the keeper caught in the wrong movement.

No matter how you look at it, he's too late.

Thwack-!!

The net bulges greatly.

It was my repayment to Coach Luca.

*

Director Paolo has a bad back.

So when he sits in the stands, he always crosses his legs. He did the same today.

Until the game started.

"..."

But now.

Director Paolo is not crossing his legs.

His back is also not leaning against the backrest.

He leans forward, busily writing something in the notebook he always carries, watching the game. He looks almost as if he's about to be sucked into the field.

Thwack-!!

"Wowwww-!"

"SÌÌÌÌÌÌ-!"

The sound of the net bulging is heard. At the same time, the kids' cheers erupt.

It's a goal. A goal. It's a goal for Fiorentina.

"..."

Director Paolo looks at his wristwatch.

Let's see...

30 minutes.

30 minutes have passed since the start of the game.

And that was the third goal.

Yes. There had already been two goals that shook the net.

And at the center of them all was always that kid.

"Gian Lee..."

The first goal was all Lee Gian from start to finish. He took the ball from the halfway line, created space with organic pass play, and then opened up a chance with a bold dribble break to finish it off.

The second goal showcased his vision.

The approach to the box was similar to the first goal, but this time he cut into the center.

Then he looked to the opposite side. Using the defense's focus on him, he put in a lobbing pass.

It was an impressive vision, and the quality of the pass was top-notch.

And just now, the third goal.

It was... beautiful.

The opponent, already unable to do anything after two goals, could only retreat further.

In front of them, Lee Gian boldly drew a large arc.

A mid-range shot. The beautifully curved mid-range shot was sucked right into the corner of the goal.

In just 30 minutes.

Lee Gian was making the kids from Empoli lose their will to fight.

"Ugh..."

Director Paolo groans and leans back against the backrest. He feels slightly dizzy, whether from the heat or something else. It made him forget about his aching back.

...He could use a cup of cold water to clear his head.

"Hoo..."

Director Paolo wipes the sweat from his forehead and pulls his bag from the seat next to him.

He rummages through the bag and pulls out a somewhat worn notebook.

Opening the notebook, he squints and starts looking for something.

"Let's see... Fe... Fe..."

His hand, scanning through the densely written letters, stops on one page.

"...Here it is. Federico Chiesa."

Federico Chiesa.

A product of Fiorentina's youth system and currently playing for Juventus, he's one of Italy's rising stars.

Director Paolo's notebook contains the details of Chiesa's salary negotiations.

-2015 salary per week: 192 euros.

-2016 salary per week: 2,385 euros.

-2017 salary per week: 13,865 euros.

-2018 salary per week: 60,538 euros.

As Director Paolo reads through those records, Coach Tony's words come to mind.

"Today is the cheapest."

Pffft- Director Paolo lets out a chuckle and starts making an urgent phone call.

It seems he'll need more funds.
